Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lets analyze the activities and their logical flow in an order fulfillment process RO Receive Order CA Check Availability RI Reserve Items PI Pick Items QC Quality Check PO Pack Order GSL Generate Shipping Label D Dispatch IC Invoice Customer RP Record Payment Logical order of activities 1 RO must always be first 2 CA must happen after RO and before RI PI etc 3 RI must happen after CA 4 PI must happen after RI 5 QC must happen after PI 6 PO must happen after QC 7 GSL must happen after PO or around PO 8 D must happen after PO and GSL 9 IC must happen after RO maybe after D but usually after order is dispatched or packaged Lets say IC happens after D or at least after PO 10 RP must happen after IC

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets define the constraints Equivalence Activities that always occur the same number of times RO CA RI PI QC PO GSL D IC RP If it is a standard happy path each activity happens exactly once Always Before If RI occurs CA must have occurred previously RI CA If PI occurs RI must have occurred previously PI RI If QC occurs PI must have occurred previously QC PI etc Always After If RO occurs CA occurs afterwards RO CA If CA occurs RI occurs afterwards CA RI etc Never Together no obvious ones Activity Occurrences RO 1 CA 1 RI 1 PI 1 QC 1 PO 1 GSL 1 D 1 IC 1 RP 1 Directly Follows RO CA CA RI RI PI QC PO PO GSL GSL D

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Activity Occurrences Bounds the number of occurrences Lets vary it a bit to make it realistic Maybe QC can be 0 or 1 sometimes skipped Maybe PI can be 1 or 2 if split Lets do RO 1 CA 1 RI 1 PI 12 QC 01 PO 1 GSL 1 D 1 IC 1 RP 1

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: if QC can be 01 then PI might not always be followed by QC If QC is 0 PO happens after PI If QC is 1 PO happens after QC Lets just make all frequencies 1 to make it simpler and perfectly consistent Lets stick to QC 1 for simplicity and robust logic

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets double check the definitions Equivalence If the first activity occurs then it has the same occurrences as the second one Always Before If the first activity occurs then the second activity should have been executed previously Always After If the first activity occurs then the second activity is executed in one of the following events Directly Follows If the first activity occurs then the second activity shall occur immediately after Lets double check if always_before pair order is correct If CA occurs RO should have been executed previously CA RO is correct If RI occurs CA should have been executed previously RI CA is correct Lets double check always_after pair order If RO occurs CA is executed after RO CA is correct

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Equivalence RO CA both happen once PI PO both happen once GSL D both happen once IC RP both happen once Always Before CA RO RI CA PI RI QC PI PO QC GSL PO D GSL RP IC all correct per definition Always After RO CA CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D IC RP all correct Directly follows RO CA CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D D IC IC RP all match sequential flow
